title: loxocarya benthamii
intro: Loxocarya benthamii, also known as Bentham's spike-grass or spike-rush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Restionaceae, commonly found in Australia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Loxocarya benthamii include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as combat infections. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional or traditional healer.
